text.tspan: PROBLEM: multiple replicas of the same data, edited CONCURRENTLY without coordination.
E.g., your Google Doc: you and a colleague type at the same time. Both edits must merge.
E.g., your shopping cart: synced across phone and laptop while offline; reconnect u2192 must merge.
CAN'T use lock + leader (latency, single point of failure, offline impossible).
CAN'T accept 'last write wins' for everything (loses concurrent edits).
CRDT IDEA: design data types whose merge function is
If merge has these properties, all replicas EVENTUALLY CONVERGE
to the same value, no matter the order or path of message delivery.
Merge = a function that takes two states, returns combined.
The merge must be a 'join semi-lattice'.
Replicas exchange OPERATIONS via reliable broadcast.
